-   they are <u><link="engaged_in_combat">Engaged in Combat</link></u>, or
-   they are already <u><link="rulebook-shaken">Shaken</link></u>, or
-   they already passed a Panic Test during this phase.
If a unit fails a Panic test, it immediately becomes <u><link="rulebook-shaken">Shaken</link></u>.
Note that this normally does not mean the unit performs a <u><link="flee_move">Flee Move</link></u>.

Name: Feigned Flight
Description:
At the end of a Round of Combat, if a unit entirely with <u><link="feigned_flight">Feigned Flight</link></u> is still Engaged with an enemy unit, before rolling any Break Tests, any unit entirely with <u><link="feigned_flight">Feigned Flight</link></u> may choose to voluntarily break from the Combat. If so, the unit is considered to have failed a Break Test. If the unit was on the winning side of the Combat, the enemy cannot Pursue.
In addition, any <u><link="rally_test">Rally Tests</link></u> taken by units entirely with <u><link="feigned_flight">Feigned Flight</link></u> after voluntarily becoming <u><link="rulebook-shaken">Shaken</link></u>, are automatically passed. This cannot be used if the unit has performed another <u><link="flee_move">Flee move</link></u> before rolling the <u><link="rally_test">Rally Test</link></u>.
<u><link="model">Models</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-fearless">Fearless</link></u> and <u><link="feigned_flight">Feigned Flight</link></u> are not prevented from choosing Charge Reaction Flee! due to <u><link="rulebook-fearless">Fearless</link></u>.
Remember that a voluntary <u><link="flee_move">Flee Move</link></u> does not cause a Panic Test in friendly units that the Fleeing unit moves through.

Name: Engaged in Combat
Description: Once a unit successfully completes a <u><link="charge_move">Charge Move</link></u>, the Charging Unit and the target unit are <u><link="engaged_in_combat">Engaged in Combat</link></u>, and remain <u><link="engaged_in_combat">Engaged in Combat</link></u> as long as at least their Unit Boundaries are in contact with an enemy Unit Boundary.

Name: Movement Phase
Description:
Positioning your forces well is the cornerstone of effective military strategy. The Movement Phase is a crucial opportunity to redeploy each of your units to the place where it can do the most good, setting up your attacks, bombardments, feints and cunning ploys to pay off in subsequent phases and turns. Troops in military formation must learn a range of orderly manoeuvres to accomplish such movements as efficiently as possible.
In the Movement Phase, you can move your units one by one in any order. Choose a unit and perform a Move Manoeuvre. Repeat this for all your eligible and willing units. However, there are certain restrictions on which units can be moved during the Movement Phase. The following units are not allowed to move:
-   Units that attempted to charge in the current Player Turn.
-   Units that are Engaged in Combat.
-   Units that are Shaken.
-   Units that have been deployed with Ambush in this Player Turn.
/section{manoeuvres_types}/section{move_manoeuvres}

Name: Unit Spacing
Description:
Normally, units must maintain a distance of more than 1" from <u><link="impassable_terrain">Impassable Terrain</link></u> and other units, both friends and enemies. However, during ongoing moves, the <u><link="unit_spacing">Unit Spacing</link></u> requirement is temporarily lowered to 0.5", but 1" must be maintained at the end of the move (i.e. units are allowed to be within 1", but outside 0, 5" of other units and <u><link="impassable_terrain">Impassable Terrain</link></u> during a move, but they are not allowed: to come into base contact, overlap with their bases, nor to voluntarily end the move within 1" of either <u><link="impassable_terrain">Impassable Terrain</link></u> nor other units).
During moves where the owning player of the unit does not have full control of the unit, including <u><link="charge_moves">Charge Moves</link></u>, <u><link="failed_charge_move">Failed Charge Moves</link></u>, <u><link="flee_move">Flee Moves</link></u>, and <u><link="pursue_moves">Pursue Moves</link></u>, the unit is allowed to approach, and end the move, closer than 0.5" of units and <u><link="impassable_terrain">Impassable Terrain</link></u>, but it is not allowed to come into contact or overlap with their bases (except <u><link="charge_moves">Charge Moves</link></u>, which can move into contact with the target of the charge).
If a unit moves closer than 1" as a result of an uncontrolled move, such as during a <u><link="charge_move">Charge Move</link></u>, the unit is allowed to end the move closer than 1" away and is allowed to stay within 1" until the point at which it is outside 1" again.
Units are only allowed to move into base contact with another unit during specific circumstances, the most common  being a <u><link="charge_move">Charge Move</link></u>, where units are allowed to move into contact with other units, except enemy units that were not the target of the charge. This includes uncontrolled move, such as when moving a random distance (e.g. <u><link="model">models</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-random_movement">Random Movement</link></u> or when performing a <u><link="failed_charge_move">Failed Charge Move)</link></u>. Unless explicitly noted, units are not allowed to move into base contact with other units, not even temporarily.
When units end up within 1" of each as a result of unintended circumstances, or uncontrolled moves, these units are allowed to remain within 1" until the units are separated by more than 1", at which point the normal <u><link="unit_spacing">Unit Spacing</link></u> rules apply again. Removing a unit from the battlefield, such as during a Reform, does not count as moving the unit farther away for this purpose, unless the unit is actually placed back further away than 1".

Name: Advance Move
Description:
When performing an <u><link="advance_move">Advance Move</link></u>, execute the following actions in the order given below:
1 -  <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u>: The <u><link="advance_move">Advance Move</link></u> is initiated with a <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u>. At the end of this <u><link="pivot">pivot</link></u>, the unit must follow the <u><link="unit_spacing">Unit Spacing</link></u> rule (note that since this is partway through a move, the unit may normally be up to within 0.5" from other units).
2 -  <u><link="snaking_forward_move">Snaking Forward Move</link></u> (Mob): After the <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u>, the unit may do  any number of <u><link="snaking_forward_move">Snaking Forward Moves</link></u>, with a distance no more than equal to its Mobility (in inches). While performing these <u><link="snaking_forward_move">Snaking Forward Moves</link></u>, the direction the unit is facing must stay within 90 degrees (in either direction) from the direction the unit was facing after the <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u>.
3 -  Reorder: <u><link="model">Models</link></u> may be repositioned within the unit, as long as the number of files does not change.

Name: Dice
Description:
In this game, six-sided <u><link="dice">dice</link></u> are used for determining random outcomes. These <u><link="dice">dice</link></u> are referred to as a ‘D6’, and the following different types <u><link="dice">dice</link></u> rolls are used in the game:
-   Specific value: When the result depends on whether the rolled value meets a specific value, this is noted with single quotation marks around the value, such as ‘1’.
-   Higher or equal to: When the result depends on rolling equal to or higher than a specific target, this is denoted with a plus sign, such as 3+, meaning 3 or higher.
-   Multiple <u><link="dice">Dice</link></u>: Sometimes multiple <u><link="dice">dice</link></u> are rolled together, indicated by a number preceding the <u><link="dice">dice</link></u> type, 3D6.
-   Modified <u><link="dice">Dice</link></u> Rolls: <u><link="dice">Dice</link></u> rolls can be modified in the following ways:
-   Adding a number; D6+2
-   Subtracting a number; D6-2
-   Multiplying with a number: D6×2
-   Natural Roll: The value of a <u><link="dice">dice</link></u> roll before applying any <u><link="modifiers">modifiers</link></u> is referred to as the natural roll.
-   Maximised and Minimised Roll: Roll an additional <u><link="dice">dice</link></u>, and discard the lowest or highest rolled <u><link="dice">dice</link></u> before applying <u><link="modifiers">modifiers</link></u> or adding up results.
-   Maximised: Discard the lowest rolled <u><link="dice">dice</link></u>.
-   Minimised: Discard the highest rolled <u><link="dice">dice</link></u>.
-   Both:
This rule can stack. However, if both Maximised and Minimised are applied to the same roll, each instance of one cancels out an instance of the other. This means that only multiples of the same (ie. all Maximised or all Minimised) will actually be rolled in your hand.

-   Rerolling <u><link="dice">Dice</link></u>: Certain effects allow for rerolling specific <u><link="dice">dice</link></u> once. This means picking up the <u><link="dice">dice</link></u> that meets the reroll criteria and rolling them again, with the second result being final (i.e. a rerolled <u><link="dice">dice</link></u> can never be rerolled again).
-   Rolling a D3: A D3  is a 3-sided <u><link="dice">dice</link></u> that will randomise a number between 1 - 3. This can be simulated by halving the result of a D6 roll, rounding up. This achieves a result from 1 - 3.

Name: Defender's Advantage
Description: The Defender must choose a single of its Deployed units to gain <u><link="rulebook-vanguard">Vanguard(3")</link></u>. This unit cannot contain any <u><link="characters">Characters</link></u>. Remember a unit that already has <u><link="rulebook-vanguard">Vanguard</link></u> may still be chosen, the effects are just not cumulative.

Name: Pursue Moves
Description:
Before moving any units, check:
-   Which Pursuing unit would Charge an enemy unit (see below). Ignore other Pursuing units.
-   Which Arc the Pursuing unit is Located in for each enemy unit that may be Charged later in this process. If the Pursuit Move will lead to a Charge, it will be in the Facing determined at this point.
The Charging units will be moved first, in the order that best satisfies the <u><link="combined_charges">Combined Charges</link></u> rules (see <u><link="charge_phase">Charge Phase</link></u> chapter). Afterwards the remaining Pursuing units will be moved, in an order chosen by their owner.
1. <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u>
If the unit is performing an Overrun, ignore this step; the unit always moves straight forward without <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u>ing.
The Pursuing unit <u><link="pivot">Pivots</link></u> so that it is facing the same direction as the Pursued unit, or if  <u><link="removed">removed</link></u> as a casualty, the direction the Pursued unit would have had, had it not been  <u><link="removed">removed</link></u> as a casualty. Ignore the <u><link="unit_spacing">Unit Spacing</link></u> rule during this <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u>.
After the <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u>, one of the four situations below will arise. If more than one is applicable, apply the uppermost one.
-   Enemy unit: If the Front Facing of the Pursuing unit would be in contact with the Unit Boundary of an enemy unit that did not Flee from the same Combat, it declares a Charge against that unit, which can not perform any <u><link="charge_reactions">Charge Reactions</link></u>.  If there is more than one possible target, the <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u>ing unit chooses which unit to Charge.
To perform the <u><link="charge_move">Charge move</link></u>, Remove the Pursuing unit from the Battlefield, and then place it back following the rules below:
-   It cannot change formation, 
-   Its Front Facing is in contact with its target Facing determined before the <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u>, 
-   The centre of the Pursuing unit moves as short a distance as possible from its position before the <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u>, while still allowing a legal position following the <u><link="unit_spacing">Unit Spacing</link></u> Rule after the Align Move.
Afterwards, perform the initial Align Move (see <u><link="charge_phase">Charge Phase</link></u> chapter). If there is not enough room to place the Pursuing unit in contact, treat the enemy unit as an Obstruction instead.
-   <u><link="table_edge">Table Edge</link></u>: If the Front Facing of the unit is in contact with, or outside, the <u><link="table_edge">Table Edge</link></u>, the unit performs a Post-Combat <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u> and then moves no further (i.e. ignore step 2. Forward Ahead). If it is not possible to perform this Post-Combat <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u> in a way that puts the unit in a legal end position, treat the <u><link="table_edge">Table Edge</link></u> as an Obstruction instead.
-   Obstruction: If the Front Facing of the Pursuing unit would be in contact with an Obstruction, the unit instead <u><link="pivot">Pivots</link></u> so that it faces a direction as close as possible towards the same direction that the Pursued unit is facing, while following the <u><link="unit_spacing">Unit Spacing</link></u> rule (normally this means stopping 1" away from the Obstruction). The unit moves no further (i.e. ignore step 2. Forward Ahead).
-   Free to move: If the Front Facing of the Pursuing unit touches neither of the above, proceed to the 2. Forward Ahead step.

2. Forward Ahead
Without moving the Pursuing unit, check what the first obstacle <u><link="table_edge">(Table Edge</link></u>, Enemy Unit Boundary, or Obstruction) would be, within the rectangle formed by extending its Front Facing directly ahead of the unit a distance equal to the previously rolled Pursue Distance. If more than one is applicable, apply the uppermost one.
The <u><link="unit_spacing">Unit Spacing</link></u> rule is ignored when doing this check and for all movement during Forward Ahead.
-   Enemy unit: If the first obstacle would be the Unit Boundary of an enemy unit that did not Flee from the same Combat, the Pursuing unit declares a Charge against that unit using its Pursuit Distance roll as its <u><link="charge_range">Charge Range</link></u>. If there is more than one possible target, the Pursuing unit chooses which to Charge. The Charged unit may not perform any <u><link="charge_reactions">Charge Reactions</link></u>.
The Pursuing unit immediately performs a <u><link="charge_move">Charge Move</link></u> towards the previously determined Facing, following all the normal <u><link="charge_move">Charge Move</link></u> and Align Move rules described in the <u><link="charge_phase">Charge Phase</link></u> chapter, (including Unaligned Charge and <u><link="blocked_path">Blocked Path</link></u> if applicable). If the Charge is not possible to complete, the unit does not perform a <u><link="failed_charge_move">Failed Charge Move</link></u> but treats the enemy unit as Obstruction instead, see below.
If the Pursuing unit joins a Combat that has already been fought or was created during this <u><link="melee_phase">Melee Phase</link></u>, it will be resolved in the next <u><link="melee_phase">Melee Phase</link></u>, with the Charging unit still counting as Charging. If the Pursuing unit joins a Combat that was not created during this <u><link="melee_phase">Melee Phase</link></u> and that has not been fought yet, the unit will have a chance to fight and Pursue again this phase.
-   <u><link="table_edge">Table Edge</link></u>: If the first obstacle would be the <u><link="table_edge">Table Edge</link></u>, move the unit straight forward until it touches the <u><link="table_edge">Table Edge</link></u>, and then immediately perform a Post-Combat <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u>.
-   Obstruction : If the first obstacle would be an Obstruction, move the unit straight forward until just before it enters base contact with the Obstruction.
-   Free to move: If the Front Facing of the Pursuing unit touches neither of the above,  move the unit straight forward its Pursuit Distance.

3. Legal Position?
At the end of the Pursue Move, check if the unit is in a legal position following the <u><link="unit_spacing">Unit Spacing</link></u> rule. It cannot be in contact with an enemy unit it did not declare a Charge against, but can be within 0.5" of units and <u><link="impassable_terrain">Impassable Terrain</link></u> , this  includes friendly units that were part of the same Combat. If the unit is not in a legal position, at the end of the move, backtrack the move to the unit's last legal position where it follows the <u><link="unit_spacing">Unit Spacing</link></u> rule.
Example of two units
Pursuing into the same enemy unit.a) Unit D loses the Combat, Breaks, and Flees 7". The owner of the winning units chooses to roll for unit A’s Pursuit Distance first. 6" is not enough to catch the Fleeing unit. Unit B’s Pursuit Distance is 7", so it is equal to or higher than unit D’s Flee Distance: the Fleeing unit is immediately  <u><link="removed">removed</link></u> as a casualty. Unit C's Pursuit Distance is 5".\\
b) When checking which units will Charge an enemy unit during their Pursuit, before moving any Pursuing unit, it turns out that both unit A and unit B will Charge unit E, so both units declare a Charge against unit E. Unit C will not Charge any enemy units. \\
c) Now units A and B perform their Pursuit Moves first. During this move, they can move through one another ignoring the <u><link="unit_spacing">Unit Spacing</link></u> rule. Otherwise, they move using the normal rules for <u><link="charge_moves">Charge Moves</link></u> (one Wheel allowed, Maximising Contact). In order to maximise the number of <u><link="model">models</link></u> and units in base contact, unit A aligns its Front Facing with unit E’s, while unit B moves into corner to corner contact with unit E. Afterwards unit C <u><link="pivot">Pivots</link></u> and moves its Pursuit Distance straight forward.

Name: Charge Phase
Description:
In the Charge Phase, your forces will take the initiative, breaking from their ordered manoeuvres to run headlong across the field, with the single purpose of meeting the enemy in Combat. But such charges are not so simple as they seem; eager troops can hesitate and stumble as the reality of battle approaches, or they might find their target is too far away, allowing the opponent to draw them out of position or rake them with fire before they can reach their ranks.The Charge Phase is an opportunity for the Active Player to move their units into Combat with enemy units by declaring charges. It is the primary method of engaging in Close Combat.
Never wear red to a minotaur party – that’s one of the classic blunders!
The Charge Phase is divided into the following steps:
1 -  Start of the Charge Phase (and Player Turn)
2 -  Declare Charges
-   The Active Player chooses one of their units, and Declares a Charge against an enemy unit.
-   The Reactive Player declares and resolves the target’s Charge Reaction.
-   Repeat a - b, with your units, one at a time, until all of your units that can and wish to Declare a Charge have done so.
3 -  Moves Charges
-   The Active Player chooses a unit that has Declared a Charge, and rolls its Charge Range roll. If there are multiple units charging the same target, roll for Charge Range for units that charge the same target.
-   Perform the Charge move or Failed Charge Move with the unit(s) you just rolled Charge Range for.
-   Repeat step 3 for all units that Declared a Charge this phase.
4 -  End of the Charge Phase.

/section{declaring_charges}/section{charge_moves}/section{failed_charge_move}/section{engaged_in_combat}

Name: Table Edge
Description:
The <u><link="table_edge">Table Edge</link></u> represents the boundaries of the game. A unit is allowed to temporarily and partially move off the board during a Movement Manoeuvre or <u><link="charge_move">Charge move</link></u> with the following restrictions:
-   The unit's Front Facing must remain entirely on the board at all times except during align moves.
-   The unit must finish any movement with its Unit Boundary entirely on the board.

Name: Saves
Description:
When wounds are inflicted on a unit, there are two types of saves that the defender can make to potentially negate the damage: Armour Saves and Special Saves.
When taking saves, the owner of the attacked model rolls a D6 and compares it with the needed score to pass the save. See the details below for how to pass Armour Saves and Special Saves.
If successful, the wound is ignored. First roll Armour Save, and then, if this is not passed, roll the  Special Save. If the Special Save is failed, or the model does not have a Special Save, a wound is inflicted. Proceed to Losing Health Points and Casualties.

Armour Saves
The defender rolls Armour Saves, by rolling a D6 for each wound, and comparing the result with the model’s Armour Value, reduced by the Attack's Armour Penetration.
The modified Armour Value describes the number of sides on a D6, counting down from the highest value, that will result in a successful roll, see tables below. If the Armor Save is successful, the wound is ignored. However, a natural roll of ‘1’ is considered a failure.
Arm - AP    Armour Save Roll
5 or above    2+
4    3+
3    4+
2    5+
1    6+
0    Cannot be passed  
AP 0    AP 1    AP 2    AP 3    AP 4    AP 5    AP 6
Arm 0    N/A    N/A    N/A    N/A    N/A    N/A    N/A
Arm 1    6+    N/A    N/A    N/A    N/A    N/A    N/A
Arm 2    5+    6+    N/A    N/A    N/A    N/A    N/A
Arm 3    4+    5+    6+    N/A    N/A    N/A    N/A
Arm 4    3+    4+    5+    6+    N/A    N/A    N/A
Arm 5    2+    3+    4+    5+    6+    N/A    N/A
Arm 6    2+    2+    3+    4+    5+    6+    N/A  
For example, Armour 3, means that ‘6’, ‘5’, and ‘4’ would be successful Armour Save rolls. If the model is attacked by Armour Penetration 1, its Armour is reduced to Armour 2, which now means that ‘6’ and ‘5’ are successful Armour Save rolls. 
This is equivalent with using the formula below to determine the required roll to negate the wound:
7 - (Defender’s Armor)
+ (Attacker’s Armour Penetration)
Combined Armour
Each model has a base Armour value in its profile, and some models may have additional equipment, rules, or are under the effect of spells that modify the Armour values. Different sources of Armour can be combined, adding up to the total combined Armour.
For example, a model with a base Armour of 3 that is under the effect of a spell that grants +1 Armour, has a combined Armour of 4. When wounded by an attack with Armour Penetration 1, the Armour Save roll would be ‘4’, ‘5’, or ‘6’ (or 4+).
Special Saves
If a wound is not saved by the Armour Save, the defender may negate the attack with its Special Save, if it has one. Special Saves work like Armour saves, except they are unaffected by Armour Penetration.
A natural roll of ‘1’ or ‘2’ is always considered a failure.
There are two main types of Special Saves; Aegis and Regeneration, each explained in more detail in the  Model Rules section. A model is only allowed to roll one Special Save roll for each attack.

Name: Open Terrain
Description: <u><link="open_terrain">Open Terrain</link></u> normally does not have any effect on <u><link="line_of_sight">Line of Sight</link></u>, <u><link="cover">Cover</link></u>, or movement. All parts of the board that are not covered by any kind of <u><link="terrain">Terrain</link></u> Features is <u><link="open_terrain">Open Terrain</link></u>.

Name: Charge Moves
Description:
After all Charges and Charge Reactions have been declared and resolved, Charging Units will now attempt to move into Combat. Each unit that declared a Charge in this phase, now rolls its Charge Range and, if successful, performs a Charge Move. Roll and move units one by one.

Charge Range
Roll D6+4 and add the unit's Charge Speed (see Characteristic Profiles in the Definitions and Terminology chapter).
-   If the Charge Range is equal to or greater than the distance between the Charging unit and its intended target (in inches), there is enough space to complete the Charge Move including the Align Move, the Charge is successful and the Charging unit performs a Charge Move.
-   Otherwise, the Charge has failed and the Charging Unit performs a Failed Charge Move.
Front or Flank?a) The Charging unit B is Located in enemy A's Front Arc (since this is where the centre of its Front Facing is).\
b) The Charging unit must contact the charged unit's Front Facing.
Some players prefer to know their odds when declaring charges.\
Below are the probabilities for the most common charges.
Needed score to & 5" & 6" & 7" & 8" & 9" & 10"
D6+4 & 100% & 83% & 67% & 50% & 33% & 17%
Maximised*D6+4 & 100% & 97% & 89% & 75% & 56% & 31%
*Maximised rolls are explained in the Definitions and Terminiology chapter.
Charge Move
A Charge Move is divided into two moves, first a Snaking Forward Move to get into contact with the target unit, which is then followed by an Align Move.
Start with establishing in what Arc of the target unit, that the centre of the Charging Unit’s Front Facing is located in (see figure above). The Facing that corresponds to this Arc is the Target Facing. See Definitions and Terminology chapter for definitions of Arcs and Facings.
A Charge Move is then resolved using a single Snaking Forward Move (see Movement Phase chapter for details), with an unlimited distance. During this move, the Charging Unit must move contact with its intended target - The Charging Unit’s Front Facing against the Target Facing.
Charge Moves are allowed to end the move within 1" of Impassable Terrain or other units, but are not allowed to move into contact with any enemy unit, other than the target of the Charge.
Once the Charging Unit touches its Target Facing, it performs an Align Move. This is done by picking up the Charging Unit and placing it back on the board aligned with the target unit, in a way that keeps the initial point of contact on the charged unit still in contact, while maximising the number of models in contact. See the Reference Handbook for details on how to prioritise the Align Move in complex situations.
Apply the rules below when performing Align Moves. The Charge Move must be made in such a way that the Charging Unit can satisfy the conditions below as best as possible, in decreasing order of priority:
-   The initial point of contact* on the target unit, before the Align Move, must remain in contact* after the Align Move. Note that the charging model that initially made contact is not required to remain in contact*.
-   If there is no single initial point of contact*, e.g. because the initial contact*is made along a line, the owner of the Charging Unit declares where the initial point of contact* is, among the possible points of initial contact*. This choice must be made such that the conditions below are satisfied as best as possible.
-   The Charging Unit is placed fully aligned with its Front Facing flush against the Target Facing.
-   If this is not possible, apply the rules for Unaligned Charges (see below).
-   The number of models in the target unit that are in contact* must be maximised. This includes Fighting Over Gaps.
-   The number of models in the Charging Unit that are in contact* must be maximised. This includes Fighting Over Gaps.
If it is unavoidable to break one or more of the above conditions, prioritise maintaining the higher priority conditions, even if it means breaking a greater number of conditions in total. The Charging Unit must, if able to, perform its Charge Move so that the models involved in the initial contact* satisfies the conditions above as best as possible. As long as all the above conditions are satisfied to the best extent possible, the owner has the freedom to choose how to perform the Align Move.
ReferenceManualColorExample of a Charge Move. From left to right.1. Declare Charge: Unit A Declares a Charge towards unit B.
2. Initial Forward: Unit A moves straight forward.
3. Wheel: The unit is rotated forward around its front right corner.
4. Concluding Forward: Unit A moves forward again, until it contacts unit B.
5. Align: Unit A is picked up and put back down in contact and fully aligned with unit B.
Now me and Fleabag, when we’re working a mark, our most reliable method is the old one-two. It’s a classic for a reason. Go straight in and try to pinch the goods on your lonesome, you’re asking for a pointed intryductshin to a boot or five. But if the hound creeps up one way, and Klepa sidles over from behind, I can often have a leeesurely peroosal of pockets and purses before they know a thing. Teamwork makes the dream work – or as we Warborn say, broodwork makes the mood work!
Unaligned Charges
If the Charging Unit cannot perform an Align Move to where it is both fully aligned with the enemy and the initial point of contact* remains in contact (Align Move conditions 1 and 2), apply the following rules instead.The Charge Move and Align Move(s) for Unaligned Charges must be made in such a way that the initial point of contact on the target unit, before the initial Align Move, must remain in contact after the initial Align Move, and at the end of both Align Moves, the Front Facing does not pass over the projected line that runs parallel with the Target Facing.
This is marked in red in figure 2, step 1.
After making sure that the Charge will be possible without breaking above condition, the Charging Unit must satisfy the conditions below as best as possible, in decreasing order of priority:
-   The initial point of contact on the target unit, before the initial Align Move, must remain in contact* after the initial Align Move.
Note that the charging model that initially made contact is not required to remain in contact.
-   Minimise the angle between the Front Facing of the Charging Unit and the Target Facing.
-   The number of models in the target unit that are in contact* must be maximised.
-   The number of models in the Charging Unit that are in contact* must be maximised.
*Contact here is referring to Base Contact between Unit Boundaries, including Fighting Over Gaps.
After performing the initial Align Move, the target unit performs a special Align Move. It is picked up and rotated around the point of contact, such that the angle between the Charging Unit's Front Facing and the Target Facing is further minimised, possibly bringing the units into being fully aligned.
Note: Any units not fully aligned must attempt to Combat Reform whenever possible. See Melee chapter for details.
Combined Charges
When more than one unit has declared a Charge against the same enemy unit, Chargers are moved in a slightly different order.
-   Roll Charge Range for each unit Charging that same target before moving any of them.
-   Decide the order that the units will be moved, both Charge Moves and failed Charge Moves. This order must be chosen so that the number of units that successfully completes a charge is as high as possible.
-   Move the units, one by one, following the normal Charge Move rules with the following exceptions:
-   Never apply the rules for Unaligned Charges or Blocked Path. If it is not possible to place the Charging Unit fully aligned with the enemy, the charge is considered impossible.
-   Each individual unit is not required to maximize the number of models in contact (conditions 4 and 5). Instead, apply these conditions at the end of all Charge Moves. This means that the total number of models from both sides are maximized after all successful charges have been performed.
-   In situations where not all the charging units can complete the Charge Move, e.g. due to other units blocking the path, such units will perform a Failed Charge move instead.

Blocked Path
To prevent abusive situations where a unit cannot Charge a nearby enemy unit due to a convoluted positioning of enemy units the following rules are applied.
Blocked Path can be applied against enemy units that fulfill all of the following criteria:
-   The enemy unit is directly in front of your unit. This means that the first obstacle (other unit or Impassable Terrain) that a straight forward move of your unit would hit is the enemy unit, and
-   Your unit would be unable to perform a Charge Move to get into contact with the Target Facing of the enemy unit.
-   The Charge is not made impossible due to friendly units Engaged with the Target Facing of the enemy unit (i.e. Blocked Path is not applicable if the Charge is made impossible due to your own units being Engaged with the Target Facing
In this situation, your unit may Declare a Charge against the enemy unit, and if the enemy does not Flee,  use the Facing that is adjecent to the Target Facing and closest to the Charging Unit (and perform a Charge Move as normal against this chosen Target Facing).

Name: Charge Move
Description:
A <u><link="charge_move">Charge Move</link></u> is divided into two moves, first a <u><link="snaking_forward_move">Snaking Forward Move</link></u> to get into contact with the target unit, which is then followed by an Align Move.
Start with establishing in what Arc of the target unit, that the centre of the Charging Unit’s Front Facing is located in (see figure above). The Facing that corresponds to this Arc is the Target Facing. See <u><link="definitions_and_terminology">Definitions and Terminology</link></u> chapter for definitions of Arcs and Facings.
A <u><link="charge_move">Charge Move</link></u> is then resolved using a single <u><link="snaking_forward_move">Snaking Forward Move</link></u> (see <u><link="movement_phase">Movement Phase</link></u> chapter for details), with an unlimited distance. During this move, the Charging Unit must move contact with its intended target - The Charging Unit’s Front Facing against the Target Facing.
<u><link="charge_moves">Charge Moves</link></u> are allowed to end the move within 1" of <u><link="impassable_terrain">Impassable Terrain</link></u> or other units, but are not allowed to move into contact with any enemy unit, other than the target of the Charge.
Once the Charging Unit touches its Target Facing, it performs an Align Move. This is done by picking up the Charging Unit and placing it back on the board aligned with the target unit, in a way that keeps the initial point of contact on the charged unit still in contact, while maximising the number of <u><link="model">models</link></u> in contact. See the Reference Handbook for details on how to prioritise the Align Move in complex situations.
Apply the rules below when performing Align Moves. The <u><link="charge_move">Charge Move</link></u> must be made in such a way that the Charging Unit can satisfy the conditions below as best as possible, in decreasing order of priority:
-   The initial point of contact* on the target unit, before the Align Move, must remain in contact* after the Align Move. Note that the charging <u><link="model">model</link></u> that initially made contact is not required to remain in contact*.
-   If there is no single initial point of contact*, e.g. because the initial contact*is made along a line, the owner of the Charging Unit declares where the initial point of contact* is, among the possible points of initial contact*. This choice must be made such that the conditions below are satisfied as best as possible.
-   The Charging Unit is placed fully aligned with its Front Facing flush against the Target Facing.
-   If this is not possible, apply the rules for <u><link="unaligned_charges">Unaligned Charges</link></u> (see below).
-   The number of <u><link="model">models</link></u> in the target unit that are in contact* must be maximised. This includes Fighting Over Gaps.
-   The number of <u><link="model">models</link></u> in the Charging Unit that are in contact* must be maximised. This includes Fighting Over Gaps.
If it is unavoidable to break one or more of the above conditions, prioritise maintaining the higher priority conditions, even if it means breaking a greater number of conditions in total. The Charging Unit must, if able to, perform its <u><link="charge_move">Charge Move</link></u> so that the <u><link="model">models</link></u> involved in the initial contact* satisfies the conditions above as best as possible. As long as all the above conditions are satisfied to the best extent possible, the owner has the freedom to choose how to perform the Align Move.
ReferenceManualColorExample of a <u><link="charge_move">Charge Move</link></u>. From left to right.1. Declare Charge: Unit A Declares a Charge towards unit B.
2. Initial Forward: Unit A moves straight forward.
3. Wheel: The unit is rotated forward around its front right corner.
4. Concluding Forward: Unit A moves forward again, until it contacts unit B.
5. Align: Unit A is picked up and put back down in contact and fully aligned with unit B.
Now me and Fleabag, when we’re working a mark, our most reliable method is the old one-two. It’s a classic for a reason. Go straight in and try to pinch the goods on your lonesome, you’re asking for a pointed intryductshin to a boot or five. But if the hound creeps up one way, and Klepa sidles over from behind, I can often have a leeesurely peroosal of pockets and purses before they know a thing. Teamwork makes the dream work – or as we Warborn say, broodwork makes the mood work!

Name: War Platform
Description:
The <u><link="model">model</link></u> follows the rules for <u><link="attachable_models">Attachable Models</link></u>, with the following exceptions:

-   If can never perform <u><link="rulebook-make_way">Make Way</link></u> moves, not even if it is also a Character.
-   For the purpose of allocating Ranged <u><link="attacks">Attacks</link></u> onto a unit with a <u><link="rulebook-war_platform">War Platform</link></u> joined to it, the <u><link="model">model</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-war_platform">War Platform</link></u> is considered to be the same Height as the Rank-and-File <u><link="model">models</link></u> in the same unit. If there are no Rank-and-File <u><link="model">models</link></u> in the unit, ignore this rule.
-   Only a single <u><link="model">model</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-war_platform">War Platform</link></u> can be joined to the same unit.
When the <u><link="model">model</link></u> is joined to a unit, its Mobility and Charge Speed is set to that of its unit, applying the following restrictions:

-   If there are no Rank-and-File <u><link="model">models</link></u> from the unit in contact with the <u><link="rulebook-war_platform">War Platform’s</link></u> flank Facings (right and left side), the <u><link="model">model</link></u> must use its own Mobility and Charge Speed instead Meaning that, if slower than the unit, the unit’s Mobility and Charge Speed are set to those of the <u><link="rulebook-war_platform">War Platform</link></u>.
-   The <u><link="rulebook-war_platform">War Platform</link></u> cannot use the unit’s Mobility and Charge Speed values when leaving the unit, including charging out of it. Instead, it uses its own Mobility and Charge Speed.

Name: Regeneration
Description:
<u><link="rulebook-regeneration">Regeneration</link></u> is a Special Save, with a value indicated in brackets (X).
If X is given as a modifier and with a maximum value (e.g. +2), the <u><link="model">model</link></u> gains this as a modifier to all its <u><link="rulebook-regeneration">Regeneration</link></u> Special Save rolls, which cannot be increased to rolls better than 3+. If the <u><link="model">model</link></u> does not have <u><link="rulebook-regeneration">Regeneration</link></u>, it instead gains a <u><link="rulebook-regeneration">Regeneration</link></u>(7  X+). For example, a <u><link="model">model</link></u> without <u><link="rulebook-regeneration">Regeneration</link></u> that gains <u><link="rulebook-regeneration">Regeneration(+2)</link></u> will gain <u><link="rulebook-regeneration">Regeneration(5+)</link></u>.
<u><link="rulebook-regeneration">Regeneration</link></u> <u><link="saves">Saves</link></u> cannot be taken against <u><link="attacks">attacks</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-flaming_attacks">Flaming Attacks</link></u>, nor against <u><link="attacks">attacks</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-lethal_strike">Lethal Strike</link></u> that roll a natural ‘6’ to wound.

Name: Hatred
Description: An attack with <u><link="rulebook-hatred">Hatred</link></u> must reroll failed to-hit rolls.

Name: Wizard Apprentice
Description: The <u><link="model">model</link></u> becomes a Wizard, knows 1 Learned Spell, which is the Apprentice Spell (#1) of the Path it has chosen. It may do certain spell changes during <u><link="pregame_selections">Pre-game Selections</link></u>. See Battle Preparation chapter for details.

Name: Channel
Description:
During <u><link="siphon_the_veil">Siphon the Veil</link></u>, each of the Active Player’s <u><link="model">models</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-channel">Channel</link></u> may gain ‘X’ Magic <u><link="dice">Dice</link></u>.
Note, <u><link="rulebook-shaken">Shaken</link></u> <u><link="model">models</link></u> may not use <u><link="rulebook-channel">Channel</link></u>.

Name: Spear
Description:
Description: Models with <u><link="rulebook-centaurod">Centauroid</link></u> or <u><link="rulebook-mount">Mount</link></u> cannot use a Spear.

Characteristics Modifiers: +1 Agi, +1 AP, +1 additional AP towards <u><link="rulebook-centaurod">Centauroid</link></u> or <u><link="rulebook-mount">Mount</link></u>

Model Rules: <u><link="rulebook-fight_in_extra_rank">Fight in Extra Rank</link></u>

Name: Fight in Extra Rank
Description: <u><link="model">Model</link></u> parts with <u><link="rulebook-fight_in_extra_rank">Fight in Extra Rank</link></u>, or using a weapon with <u><link="rulebook-fight_in_extra_rank">Fight in Extra Rank</link></u>, can make Supporting <u><link="attacks">Attacks</link></u> from an additional rank.

Name: Extra Support
Description: The <u><link="model">model</link></u> part replaces the limit on the maximum number of <u><link="attacks">Attacks</link></u> that can be done as Restricted <u><link="attacks">Attacks</link></u> with the value in brackets (X), or if it includes a +, increases the limit with +X.

Name: Distracting
Description:
<u><link="melee_attacks">Melee Attacks</link></u> allocated against a Health Pool with <u><link="rulebook-distracting">Distracting</link></u> suffer X to hit, up to at worst 5+.
If a Max. Y apply this following the normal rules for limits defined in  the Reference Handbook.

Name: Supernal
Description:
When a unit entirely with <u><link="rulebook-supernal">Supernal</link></u> fails a Break Test, instead of being Broken and subsequently <u><link="rulebook-shaken">Shaken</link></u>, it suffers a number of Health Point losses equal to the amount by which the combat was lost, with no <u><link="saves">saves</link></u> of any kind allowed.
These Health Point losses are first allocated towards Rank-and-File <u><link="model">models</link></u>, and once enough Health Points losses are suffered to remove all Rank-and-File <u><link="model">models</link></u>, remaining Health Point losses are allocated by the owner towards <u><link="attachable_models">Attachable Models</link></u>, as evenly as possible.
<u><link="attachable_models">Attachable Models</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-supernal">Supernal</link></u> can only join units containing <u><link="rulebook-supernal">Supernal</link></u>, and units containing <u><link="rulebook-supernal">Supernal</link></u> can only be joined by <u><link="attachable_models">Attachable Models</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-supernal">Supernal</link></u>.

Name: Feigned Flight
Description:
A unit entirely with Feigned Flight automatically passes any <u><link="rulebook-rally_test">Rally Test</link></u> it takes after voluntarily becoming <u><link="rulebook-shaken">Shaken</link></u>.
In addition, models with <u><link="rulebook-fearless">Fearless</link></u> and Feigned Flight ignore Fearless for the purposes of choosing ’Flee!’ as a <u><link="rulebook-charge">Charge</link></u> reaction, i.e. if every model with <u><link="rulebook-fearless">Fearless</link></u> in a (combined) unit has Feigned Flight, the unit can <u><link="rulebook-flee">’Flee!’</link></u>.

Name: Hit and Run
Description:
A unit entirely with Hit and Run in contact with an enemy unit may choose to voluntarily become “Broken” at the start of ‘Take Break Tests’ (step 4.C of a Round of Combat). In that case, the unit is considered to have failed a Break Tet and is ignored for the rest of step 4.C.
If the unit was on the winning side of the Round of Combat, the enemy cannot <u><link="rulebook-pursue_moves">Pursue</link></u>.
Note: this means units with <u><link="rulebook-supernal">Supernal</link></u> or <u><link="rulebook-unstable">Unstable</link></u> suffer no Health Point loss if they voluntarily become “Broken”.

Name: Magical Attacks
Description: This Attack Attribute does not confer any direct effects, but instead interacts with other rules, such as Aegis(X, against Magical Attacks).

Name: Spear of Infinity (DL)
Description:
Casting Value: 6
Range: 24"
Duration: Instant
Type: Damage, Hex, Missile, Replicable

Effect:
The target suffers 1 hit with Str 2, AP 2, <u><link="rulebook-area_attack">Area Attack</link></u> (1x5), <u><link="rulebook-direct_hit">Direct Hit</link></u> (Str 4, AP 3, <u><link="rulebook-multiple_wounds">Multiple Wounds</link></u> (2)), and <u><link="rulebook-magical_attacks">Magical Attacks</link></u>.
Hits from Spear of Infinity gain +1 Str for each spell successfully cast before this spell in this Magic Phase (including other instances of Spear of Infinity).

Name: Know Thyself
Description: At the beginning of the Agility Step when the model’s <u><link="rulebook-standard_melee_attacks">Standard Melee Attacks</link></u> are allocated, select one model part from each enemy model in contact with this model. Add their Attack Value Characteristics, excluding any modifiers, to the Attack Value of the Kuulima’s Deceiver. Duration: Round of Combat.

Name: Path of Destruction
Description: The model part can allocate its <u><link="rulebook-stomp_attack">Stomp Attack</link></u> onto Health Pools of Height 4 or less (rather than 0-1). If the model part’s <u><link="rulebook-stomp_attack">Stomp Attack</link></u> is allocated towards a Health Pool with Height (3–4), the total number of hits is halved, rounding fractions up.

Name: Devour
Description:
For each Health Point loss the model causes with <u><link="rulebook-melee_attacks">Melee Attacks</link></u>, roll a D6 at the end of the Agility Step. For each 4+ rolled, the model gains +1 Health Point.
If the model’s Health Points reach 20 or more, all units within 9" immediately suffer 2D6 hits with <u><link="rulebook-toxic_attacks">Toxic Attacks</link></u>, and the model is removed as a casualty.

Name: Bribed Enchantments
Description: If the model is in contact with an enemy unit containing a Magic Item during Step2.b of each Round of Combat (‘Calculate Static Combat Score’), add+1to Combat Score.

Name: Abandon All Hope
Description:
During Tristitia's Shooting Phase, they can make a Shooting Attack that targets all enemy units within 12" of Tristitia (including those <u><link="rulebook-engaged_in_combat">Engaged in Combat</link></u> and outside <u><link="rulebook-arcs">Front Arc</link></u> and/or <u><link="rulebook-line_of_sight">Line of Sight</link></u>).
Each target suffers 1 hit for each Rank it has, with Strength 9, Armour Penetration 10, <u><link="rulebook-magical_attacks">Magical Attacks</link></u> and <u><link="rulebook-psychic_attacks">Psychic Attacks</link></u>. This attack can be used even if Tristitia is <u><link="rulebook-engaged_in_combat">Engaged in Combat</link></u>.

Name: Growing Despair
Description: <u><link="rulebook-afflict">Afflict</link></u> (−1 Dis, −X Def, and −X Off), where ’X’ is equal to the number of the current Game Turn.

Name: Resistance
Description: <u><link="attacks">Attacks</link></u> allocated against the <u><link="model">model’s</link></u> Health Pool must reroll successful <u><link="towound_rolls">to-wound rolls</link></u>.

Name: Musician
Description:
Musician is a unit-wide upgrade, normally represented by the presence of a regimental musician.
When performing a <u><link="rulebook-reform_move">Reform Move</link></u> or Combat Reform, units containing a Musician</link></u> may change their files by ±5 rather than the normal ±1.
In addition, units containing Musician benefit from a +1 Discipline modifier when taking Command Tests.

Name: Razor Tentacles
Description: The model gains <u><link="rulebook-zeal">Zeal</link></u> while Engaged with the Flank or Rear Facing of an enemy unit.

Name: Divine Right
Description:
During Combat Choices, the opponent must select a Character from one of their units that is in contact with the model's unit. The selected Character and the model with <u>Divine Right</u> count as being in contact with each other.
In addition, while the model with Divine Right is in contact with an enemy Character, do not roll for determining its stats: all random characteristics are instead considered to have rolled the maximum possible value.

Name: Multiple Wounds
Description:
Unsaved wounds caused by the attack are multiplied into the value given in brackets (X). If the value is a <u><link="dice">dice</link></u>, roll one <u><link="dice">dice</link></u> for each unsaved wound from an attack with <u><link="rulebook-multiple_wounds">Multiple Wounds</link></u>. The amount of wounds that the attack is multiplied into can never be higher than the Health Points Characteristic of the target, excluding Health Points lost previously in the battle.
For example, if a <u><link="rulebook-multiple_wounds">Multiple Wounds(D6)</link></u> attack wounds a unit of Trolls (HP 3) and rolls a ‘5’ for the multiplier, the number of unsaved wounds is reduced to 3, even if the Troll unit has already lost one or two Health Points previously in battle.

Name: Wizard Master
Description: The <u><link="model">model</link></u> becomes a Wizard, gains <u><link="rulebook-channel">Channel(2)</link></u> and knows 5 Learned <u><link="spells">Spells</link></u>; chosen from the Apprentice Spell, the Adept, and Master <u><link="spells">Spells</link></u> (#1 - #6) of the Path it has chosen. It may do certain spell changes during <u><link="pregame_selections">Pre-game Selections</link></u>. See Battle Preparation chapter for details.

Name: Omniscience
Description: If one or more models with Omniscience have neither Marched nor Charged in this Player Turn, the owner's limit on Excess Magic is increased by 2.

Name: Grind Attack
Description:
The <u><link="model">model</link></u> part can make a Special Attack which is resolved with the <u><link="model">model</link></u> part’s Agility, and inflicts X hits, using the <u><link="model">model</link></u> part’s Strength, Armour Penetration, and <u><link="attack_attributes">Attack Attributes</link></u>, but not <u><link="weapons">Weapons</link></u>.
Remember that <u><link="attacks">attacks</link></u> multiplied into several hits are allocated after establishing how many hits are scored (i.e. after the attack has hit).

Name: Energy Bolts
Description: Aim 4+, Range 24", Shots 1, Str 5, AP 0, <u><link="rulebook-magical_attacks">Magical Attacks</link></u>

Name: Smother
Description: <u><link="rulebook-melee Attacks">Melee Attacks</link></u> towards a model with Height (X) gain +1 to hit and +1 to wound.

Name: Stand and Shoot
Description:
The <u><link="model">model</link></u> may perform an out of sequence Shooting Attack when an enemy unit has successfully performed a <u><link="charge_move">Charge Move</link></u> into contact with the <u><link="model">model’s</link></u> unit, if the following conditions are met:

-   The <u><link="model">model’s</link></u> unit is not <u><link="engaged_in_combat">Engaged in Combat</link></u> with any other units. Note that this means during a Combined Charge only the first Charging Unit moved can be targeted; except if the Charging Unit is <u><link="removed">removed</link></u> at the end of its move. As long as the Charging Unit is <u><link="removed">removed</link></u> at the end of its move, there is no limit on the amount of times a unit may use <u><link="rulebook-stand_and_shoot">Stand and Shoot</link></u>.

-   The enemy unit Charges the <u><link="model">model</link></u> in its unit’s Front Facing.
-   The enemy unit started the Charge further away than its Charge Speed, using the lowest Charge Speed in the unit.

-   The Charge was not done as part of a Pursue Move.

This is a Shooting Attack that does not require <u><link="line_of_sight">Line of Sight</link></u>, can be done even if the enemy unit is not in the Shooter's Front Arc, and is always resolved against the target unit's Front Facing.

Name: Dark Fire
Description: Aim X+, Range 18", Shots 2, Str 4, AP 0, Armour Save rolls of ‘1’, ‘2’, ‘3’, and ‘4’ are always considered failed when saving wounds caused by Dark Fire, Magical Attacks.

Name: Horror
Description:
<u><link="flee_move">Flee Moves</link></u> from enemy units made in the following situations gain Minimised two times:

-   Flee! <u><link="charge_reactions">Charge Reactions</link></u> declared in reaction to a Charge Declaration from a unit containing this rule. (Flee! <u><link="charge_reactions">Charge Reactions</link></u> declared when later charged by other units are unaffected.)
-   Breaking from combat while in contact with one or more units containing <u><link="rulebook-horror">Horror</link></u>.

Remember that units containing <u><link="rulebook-fearless">Fearless</link></u> are unaffected by this rule.

Name: Horde Thresher
Description: Changes to the model's profile: • Base size changed to 100×100 mm. • HP set to 6. • Number of Tillers and Hellsteeds is set to (3). • Tillers lose Smother (1) and gain Smother (2). • Chassis gains Impact Hits (3D3 hits). • Single model.

Name: Legion Thresher
Description: Changes to the model's profile: • Base size changed to 100×150mm. • HP set to 8. • Number of Tillers and Hellsteeds is set to (4). • Tillers lose Smother (1) and gain Smother (2). • Chassis gains Impact Hits (4D3 hits). • Single model.

Name: Mountain Breaker
Description: The model's Charge Range rolls towards units containing Height 5 are Maximised. Impact Hits gain Multiple Wounds (D3, towards Height 5).

Name: Swift Anger
Description:
Whenever the model loses a Health Point, it gains +1 Agi.
Whenever it <u><link="rulebook-recover">Recovers</link></u> or <u><link="rulebook-raise">Raises</link></u> a Health Point, it suffers -1 Agi.

Name: Trophy for the Collection
Description: When an enemy Character is removed as a casualty as a result of Melee Attacks from a model part on this model without Mount or Construct, add+3 to the model’s side’s Combat Score during this Round of Combat.

Name: Veil Stalker
Description: For every successfully cast spell in the opponents previous Magic Phase, this unit’s Ambush roll gains one instance of Maximised.

Name: Tightening Grasp
Description:
The model gains <u><link="rulebook-grind_attack">Grind Attack (X hits)</link></u>.
After each Round of Combat, 'X' increases by 1 (e.g. <u><link="rulebook-grind_attack">Grind Attack (1 hit)</link></u> becomes <u><link="rulebook-grind_attack">Grind Attack (2 hits)</link></u>). When the model is not Engaged in Combat, 'X' is set to 1.
When a model part with Tightening Grasp is in the second rank, and not in contact with any enemy model, it can make all its hits from <u><link="rulebook-grind_attack">Grind Attack</link></u> across models directly in front of it in the Front Rank.

Name: Parry
Description:
Against <u><link="melee_attacks">Melee Attacks</link></u> made by an attacker Engaged in the <u><link="model">models</link></u> Front Facing the Defensive Skill of a <u><link="model">model</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-parry">Parry</link></u> is always at least equal to the Offensive Skill of the attacker.
Remember that since <u><link="rulebook-parry">Parry</link></u> provides a modifier to a Defensive Characteristic, this is not applicable to a Character with a <u><link="mount">mount</link></u> with its own Defensive Skill.

Name: Falling Star
Description: At the beginning of ‘Determine Agility Order’ (Melee Phase, 3.A), the model's Agility is set to twice the model’s current number of Health Points. At the start of each Agility Step, the model’s Defensive Skill and Offensive Skill are set to twice the model’s current number of Health Points.

Name: Aether Battery
Description: Aim 3+, Range 18", Shots 3D6, Str 4, AP 1, If the model did not move this turn, the number of Shots gain Maximised. <u><link="rulebook-magical_attacks">Magical Attacks</link></u>

Name: Morphlings
Description:
During Pre-game Selections, each unit of Veil Serpents must choose one of the following <u><link="dl-daemonic_manifestations">Manifestations</link></u>: it gains the chosen Manifestations, with Duration: Permanent:

• <u><link="dl-mesmerising_plumage">Mesmerising Plumage</link></u>
• <u><link="dl-torporous_tendrils">Torporous Tendrils</link></u>
• <u><link="dl-venom_sacs">Venom Sacs</link></u>

Name: Acid Blood
Description: Each time a model with Acid Blood loses a Health Point after failing a <u><link="rulebook-regeneration">Regeneration</link></u> Save against an enemy <u><link="rulebook-melee_attacks">Melee Attack</link></u>, it immediately inflicts 1 hit with <u><link="rulebook-toxic_attacks">Toxic Attacks</link></u> towards the Health Pool of the attacking model, before any casualties are removed.

Name: Rally Test
Description:
During the <u><link="rally_phase">Rally Phase</link></u>, the Active Player's <u><link="rulebook-shaken">Shaken</link></u> units must take a  <u><link="command_test">Command Test</link></u>, called a <u><link="rally_test">Rally Test</link></u>. Roll tests one by one in any order. Units <u><link="engaged_in_combat">Engaged in Combat</link></u> cannot take <u><link="rally_test">Rally Tests</link></u>.
-   If the <u><link="rally_test">Rally Test</link></u> is passed, the unit stops being <u><link="rulebook-shaken">Shaken</link></u> and may immediately <u><link="pivot">Pivot</link></u> (see <u><link="movement_phase">Movement Phase</link></u> chapter).
-   If the <u><link="rally_test">Rally Test</link></u> is failed, the unit <u><link="pivot">Pivots</link></u> to face directly away from the centre of the closest non-<u><link="rulebook-shaken">Shaken</link></u> enemy unit and then performs a <u><link="flee_move">Flee Move</link></u> (see <u><link="psychology">Psychology</link></u> chapter).
Remember that <u><link="decimated">Decimated</link></u> units that perform <u><link="flee_move">Flee Moves</link></u> are <u><link="removed">removed</link></u> as <u><link="casualties">casualties</link></u>.

Name: To-Wound Rolls
Description:
When making an attack it is necessary to wound the target in order to potentially cause harm.
To determine if a hit successfully wounds the target, roll a D6 for each hit. The required roll to wound depends on the difference between the <u><link="attacks">attack’s</link></u> Strength and the defender’s Resilience, as indicated in the tables below. Both tables present the same relation, visualised in different ways.
A natural roll of ‘6’ always succeeds, while a natural roll of ‘1’ always fails. The player who made the attack rolls to determine if each hit wounds the target. A successful to-wound roll results in a wound.
If the attack does not have a Strength, refer to the specific rules provided for that particular attack.


Difference
Str - Res    Required roll to wound
2 or more    2+
1    3+
0    4+
-1    5+
-2 or less    6+  


Str 1    Str 2    Str 3    Str 4    Str 5    Str 6    Str 7    Str 8    Str 9    Str 10
Res 1    4+    3+    2+    2+    2+    2+    2+    2+    2+    2+
Res 2    5+    4+    3+    2+    2+    2+    2+    2+    2+    2+
Res 3    6+    5+    4+    3+    2+    2+    2+    2+    2+    2+
Res 4    6+    6+    5+    4+    3+    2+    2+    2+    2+    2+
Res 5    6+    6+    6+    5+    4+    3+    2+    2+    2+    2+
Res 6    6+    6+    6+    6+    5+    4+    3+    2+    2+    2+
Res 7    6+    6+    6+    6+    6+    5+    4+    3+    2+    2+
Res 8    6+    6+    6+    6+    6+    6+    5+    4+    3+    2+
Res 9    6+    6+    6+    6+    6+    6+    6+    5+    4+    3+
Res 10    6+    6+    6+    6+    6+    6+    6+    6+    5+    4+

Name: Charge Reactions
Description:
When a unit has a Charge declared against it must immediately perform a Charge Reaction. The owner of the charged unit declares which Charge Reaction, and resolve the effects immediately. There are two <u><link="charge_reactions">Charge Reactions</link></u> to choose from:
-   Hold!: This is the default Charge Reaction. Choosing the Hold! Charge Reaction means the unit stands its ground and makes no further action.
-   Flee!: A unit can select this Charge Reaction only once per phase, and only if not already <u><link="engaged_in_combat">Engaged in Combat</link></u>. The unit is pivoted to face directly away from the Centre of the charging unit, it then performs a <u><link="flee_move">Flee Move</link></u> (see <u><link="psychology">Psychology</link></u> section).
-   If this <u><link="flee_move">Flee Move</link></u> brings the fleeing unit into contact with a unit that declared a Charge against it this phase, the fleeing unit is immediately <u><link="removed">removed</link></u> as a casualty.
-   Remember <u><link="flee_move">Flee Moves</link></u> due to Charge Reaction Flee! do not cause a Panic Test in friendly units that the Fleeing unit moves through (unless caused by a failed Panic Test when charged by a unit containing <u><link="rulebook-terror">Terror)</link></u>.

Name: Chilling Yawn
Description: Enemy units in base contact with one or more models with this <u><link="manifestation">Manifestation</link></u> suffer -2 Agility.

Name: Scent of Blood
Description: Charge Range rolls made by units entirely with Scent of Blood gain Maximised if the target of the Charge contains a Health Pool that has fewer Health Points than its starting value.

Name: Meeting of Minds (DE)
Description: The model gains Scent of Blood, and treats all friendly models with Beast as Insignificant.

Name: Artistry of Death
Description: <link="rulebook-standard_melee_attacks">Standard Melee Attacks</link></u> gain +1 to wound.

Name: A Watery Grave
Description: Tethaen is always considered to have rolled a 3+ when rolling for whether he arrives using Ambush. In addition, at the beginning of "Post Combat" (Round of Combat, Step 6), if Tethaen won the Round of Combat and an enemy Character in that Combat was removed as a casualty, Tethaen may then be placed in Ambush.

Name: Barbed Nets
Description: <u><link="rulebook-afflict">Afflict</link></u> (-1 Str, -1 AP)

Name: Blessed by Harag
Description: <u><link="rulebook-aegis">Aegis</link></u> (5+), <u><link="rulebook-resistance">Resistance</link></u> (against <u><link="rulebook-flaming_attacks">Flaming Attacks</link></u>).

Name: Grim Harpoon
Description:
Counts as a Great Weapon in Melee. +1 Att.
Any unit that suffers one or more hits from this weapon loses <u><link="rulebook-swiftstride">Swiftstride</link></u>, and cannot regain it in any way, until the start of the Active Player’s next Turn.

Shooting Weapon.
Aim 3+, Range 18", Shots 1, Str 6, AP 3, <u><link="rulebook-accurate">Accurate</link></u>, <u><link="rulebook-quick_to_fire">Quick to Fire</link></u>

Name: Aembeghen's Blades
Description:
Paired Weapons.
If there are any Enemy Characters in Contact with the wielders Unit, The Black Rose gains +2 Att.
In addition, any Melee Attacks the Black Rose allocates against a Character gain +2 Str, +4 AP.

Name: Dance of the Rose
Description: During Combat Choices, The Black Rose must choose one of the following Dances, with Duration: Round of Combat. The Black Rose cannot select a specific Dance again until either or both of the following has occurred: • She is not Engaged in Combat. • She has selected all Dances. • She can only select Dance of Caedhren, and it is not possible to do the Make Way. Dances: • Dance of Caedhren: The Black Rose can use Make Way, even when in contact with enemy models, provided she moves into contact with an enemy Character in her Unit's front facing. • Dance of Cenyrn: Nominate one enemy Character model in contact with The Black Rose. The Black Rose and the selected Character always have 0 Attacks, and no attacks nor hits can be assigned to either. At Agility Step 0, The Black Rose rolls D3 with Maximized and the selected Character rolls D3. If either model rolls lower, it loses the difference number of Health Points equal to the difference between the two rolls. • Dance of Nabh: The Black Rose gains Hatred (against Characters), Zeal (against Characters). Attacks against The Black Rose gain +1 To Hit.

Name: Dance of Caedhren
Description: The Black Rose can use <u><link="rulebook-make_way">Make Way</link></u>, even when in contact with enemy models, provided she moves into contact with an enemy Character in her Unit's front facing.

Name: Dance of Cenyrn
Description: Nominate one enemy Character model in contact with The Black Rose. The Black Rose and the selected Character always have 0 Attacks, and no attacks nor hits can be assigned to either. At Agility Step 0, The Black Rose rolls D3 with Maximized and the selected Character rolls D3. If either model rolls lower, it loses the difference number of Health Points equal to the difference between the two rolls.

Name: Dance of Nabh
Description:
The Black Rose gains <u><link="rulebook-hatred">Hatred (against Characters)</link></u>, <u><link="rulebook-zeal">Zeal (against Characters)</link></u>.
Attacks against The Black Rose gain +1 To Hit.

Name: The Obsidian Thorns
Description:
The Black Rose gains <u><link="rulebook-exclusive">Exclusive (Obsidian Guard)</link></u>, <u><link="rulebook-magic_resistance">Magic Resistance (1)</link></u> and all models in her unit gain +1 Charge Speed.
In addition, Enemy Units are not allowed to choose the Flee! Charge Reaction to Charges declared on them by the Black Rose's unit, unless they are <u><link="rulebook-shaken">Shaken</link></u>.
Note: This has no influence on Charge Reactions from other Units.

Name: First Strike
Description: During the First Round of Combat, <u><link="model">model</link></u> parts with <u><link="rulebook-first_strike">First Strike</link></u>, or using a weapon with <u><link="rulebook-first_strike">First Strike</link></u>, gains the rules and Characteristic <u><link="modifiers">modifiers</link></u> stated in brackets. For example, a <u><link="model">model</link></u> part with <u><link="rulebook-first_strike">First Strike(+1 Str)</link></u> gains +1 Str in the first round of combat.

Name: Hidden
Description:
The <u><link="model">model</link></u> is not Deployed as normal. Instead, at the start of any Player Turn, the owner can choose a friendly unit containing Rank-and-File <u><link="model">models</link></u>, which satisfy the following:

-   The <u><link="model">model</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-hidden">Hidden</link></u> could normally have joined the unit.
-   The <u><link="model">model</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-hidden">Hidden</link></u> has an identical Base Size and Height as the Rank-and-File <u><link="model">models</link></u> in the unit.
-   The unit is not <u><link="rulebook-shaken">Shaken</link></u>,  is not a Summoned unit, but it can be <u><link="engaged_in_combat">Engaged in Combat</link></u>.
After choosing an eligible unit, follow these steps:

-   Remove a Rank-and-File <u><link="model">model</link></u>, with the same base size as the <u><link="model">model</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-hidden">Hidden</link></u>, from the front rank of the chosen unit as a casualty.
-   Deploy the <u><link="model">model</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-hidden">Hidden</link></u> where the <u><link="removed">removed</link></u> <u><link="model">model</link></u> was positioned.
Note: <u><link="attachable_models">Attachable models</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-hidden">Hidden</link></u> can still be Deployed using <u><link="rulebook-hidden">Hidden</link></u> rules

Name: Zeal
Description: An attack with <u><link="rulebook-zeal">Zeal</link></u> must reroll failed <u><link="towound_rolls">to-wound rolls</link></u>.

Name: Repeater Handbow
Description: Aim X+, Range 12", Shots 2, Str 3, AP 1*, Accurate, Quick to Fire, Stand and Shoot, *Attacks made at Long Range have their AP set to 0.

Name: Irresistible Will
Description: When casting a Learned spell, any Dispelling Roll made against its Casting Roll suffers a -1 modifier.

Name: Daeb Warfare
Description: When a unit containing Daeb Warfare is part of a Combined Charge, its Charge Range rolls gain Maximised. When multiple units containing Daeb Warfare are Engaged in the same Combined Combat, each of these unit gains First Strike (Resolute).

Name: March and Shoot
Description: Performing a <u><link="march_move">March Move</link></u> in the same Player Turn does not prevent shooting with <u><link="attacks">attacks</link></u> or <u><link="model">model</link></u> parts with <u><link="rulebook-march_and_shoot">March and Shoot</link></u>, but the attack can still suffer -1 to hit for Moving and Shooting.

Name: Repeater Crossbow
Description: Aim X+, Range 18", Shots 2, Str 3, AP 1*, *Attacks made at Long Range have their AP set to 0.

Name: Afflict
Description:
Units in contact with one or more enemy <u><link="model">models</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-afflict">Afflict(X)</link></u>, suffer the effects given by X.
A unit can only be affected by one instance of each <u><link="rulebook-afflict">Afflict(X)</link></u>, provided that the X in brackets is identical. If the brackets from two instances of <u><link="rulebook-afflict">Afflict</link></u> are not identical, i.e. different X, then they are considered different rules and the effects from both are applied.

Name: Kraken's Hide
Description: The wearer gains +1 Arm and <u><link="rulebook-strider">Strider (Water Terrain)</link></u>.

Name: Sweeping Attack
Description:
When a <u><link="model">model</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-sweeping_attack">Sweeping Attack</link></u> is within 1" of an unengaged enemy unit during a Move Manoeuvre (even temporarily), the <u><link="model">model’s</link></u> unit may perform a <u><link="rulebook-sweeping_attack">Sweeping Attack</link></u> against that enemy unit. This can only be used once per friendly Player Turn. Resolve the <u><link="rulebook-sweeping_attack">Sweeping Attack</link></u> when the Move Manoeuvre is completed.
The attack hits automatically, and counts as a Ranged Attack. Each <u><link="model">model</link></u> in the unit with <u><link="rulebook-sweeping_attack">Sweeping Attack</link></u> must use its <u><link="rulebook-sweeping_attack">Sweeping Attack</link></u> against the same enemy unit. The <u><link="rulebook-sweeping_attack">Sweeping Attack</link></u> hits automatically, causing a number of hits as defined in the brackets (X hits). These hits are resolved with the <u><link="model">model</link></u> part’s Strength, Armour Penetration and <u><link="attack_attributes">Attack Attributes</link></u>, but not <u><link="weapons">Weapons</link></u>.
If values for Strength, Armour Penetration and additional <u><link="attack_attributes">Attack Attributes</link></u> are defined in the bracket (Y), these are used instead. In this case, note that characteristics <u><link="modifiers">modifiers</link></u> and <u><link="attack_attributes">Attack Attributes</link></u> on the <u><link="model">model</link></u> part itself do not affect this attack.

Name: Petrifying Stare
Description: Special Attack. At the model part's Agility Step, each enemy unit in contact with the model suffers 2 hits that are set to-wound on 5+, and the hits always have AP 10, Magical Attacks and Unmodifiable.

Name: Elven Finesse
Description: A model part with this Attack Attribute attacking with a <u><link="rulebook-great_weapon">Great Weapon</link></u> strikes at -1 Agility instead of always at Agility 0.

Name: Temple Acolytes
Description: The 'Dread Acolyte' model part gains Artistry of Death, Paired Weapons and Temple.

Name: Harpoon Launcher
Description:
Aim 3+, Range 18", Shots 3, Str 6, AP 3, <u><link="rulebook-accurate">Accurate</link></u>, <u><link="rulebook-quick_to_fire">Quick to Fire</link></u>
Any unit that suffers one or more hits from this weapon loses <u><link="rulebook-swiftstride">Swiftstride</link></u>, until the start of the Active Player’s next Turn.

Name: Emplacement
Description:
Units entirely with <u><link="rulebook-emplacement">Emplacement</link></u> cannot Declare Charges, perform <u><link="march_move">March Moves</link></u> or <u><link="pursue_moves">Pursue Moves</link></u> (including Random Movement), or choose the Flee! Charge Reaction. If a unit entirely with <u><link="rulebook-emplacement">Emplacement</link></u> is about to perform a <u><link="flee_move">Flee Move</link></u>, it is instead <u><link="removed">removed</link></u> as a casualty. The <u><link="model">model</link></u> cannot shoot in the same Player Turn that it performed a Move Manoeuvre.
Units entirely with <u><link="rulebook-emplacement">Emplacement</link></u> can draw <u><link="line_of_sight">Line of Sight</link></u> in any direction from any points on their base (i.e. they are not limited by their Front Facing or Front Arc).

Name: Daeb Bolt Thrower
Description: Aim 3+, Range 24", Shots 8, Str 5, AP 2, Can shoot after Advance or Reform Move

Name: Coastal Predator
Description: The model gains <u><link="rulebook-hard_target">Hard Target (1)</link></u> while in contact with a <u><link="rulebook-water_terrain">Water Terrain</link></u> Feature.

Name: Under the Cover of Mists
Description:
Enemy units within 8" of one or more Mist Leviathans suffer -1 to hit with Shooting Attacks.
In addition, during the first Game Turn, all models in friendly units containing Height 1 within 8" of one or more Mist Leviathans gain <u><link="rulebook-hard_target">Hard Target (1)</link></u>.

Name: Water Terrain
Description:
<u><link="water_terrain">Water Terrain</link></u> can be represented in the game for example by ponds, swamps, or rivers.
<u><link="water_terrain">Water Terrain</link></u> has the following rules:
-   <u><link="dangerous_terrain">Dangerous Terrain</link></u> (6+, Metal Armour).
-   <u><link="disruptive">Disruptive</link></u>.
-   Soaked: Units in contact with <u><link="water_terrain">Water Terrain</link></u> gain <u><link="rulebook-immune">Immune</link></u> (Flaming <u><link="attacks">Attacks)</link></u>.
-   Unsure Footing: <u><link="rulebook-stomp_attack">Stomp Attacks</link></u> and <u><link="rulebook-impact_hits">Impact Hits</link></u> cannot be used by <u><link="model">models</link></u> that are in contact with <u><link="water_terrain">Water Terrain</link></u>.

Name: Ancient Grudge
Description: During Pre-game Selections, a Dwarven Holds army marks units on the opponent's Army List with Grudge Markers (note that Characters can be marked, too). The number of Grudge Markers placed is equal to the number of instances of Ancient Grudge on units in your Army List. All models with Dwarf in your army gain First Strike (Hatred (towards units with a Grudge Marker)).

Name: Dying Blow
Description: When a <u><link="model">model</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-dying_blow">Dying Blow</link></u> is about to be <u><link="removed">removed</link></u> during any Agility Step of a Round of Combat it is fighting, remove it instead as a casualty at the end of Agility Step 0.

Name: Weapon Master
Description:
The model part may choose what Melee Weapon to use in each Round of Combat, instead of only in the First Round of Combat.
In addition, it may choose to use a Hand Weapon or Innate Weapons even if it has other Melee Weapons. If armed with an Enchanted Weapon, it must still use it. (i.e. it only needs to conform to the Priority 1 of weapon choice).
All Rank-and-File models in a unit still need to choose the same weapon.

Name: Rune Craft Mastery
Description: Melee Attacks from models in a unit containing <u><link="dh-rune_craft_mastery">Rune Craft Mastery</link></u> gain +1 AP.

Name: Engineer
Description:
Once during each <u><link="shooting_phase">Shooting Phase</link></u>, a non-Shaken and Unengaged <u><link="model">model</link></u> with <u><link="rulebook-engineer">Engineer</link></u> can select a friendly <u><link="model">model</link></u> with X in a unit within 6" of it.
The chosen <u><link="model">model</link></u> gains +1 to hit with Shooting <u><link="attacks">Attacks</link></u>, to a maximum of 3+. No <u><link="model">model</link></u> can be affected by more than one instance of this effect simultaneously.
The usage of this must be declared before shooting with the chosen <u><link="model">model</link></u>.

Name: Crossbow
Description: Range 30", Shots 1, Str 4, AP 1, <u><link="rulebook-unwieldy">Unwieldy (1)</link></u>.

Name: Resolute
Description:
A unit containing Resolute and that has at least one Proper Rank is considered to have one additional <u><link="rulebook-proper_ranks">Proper Ranks</link></u> when Engaged in Combat.
Note: this is applied before applying the effects of <u><link="rulebook-surrounded">Surrounded</link></u>.

Name: Runic Anvil
Description:
The model must select three different <u><link="dh-battle_runes">Battle Runes</link></u>. Note which spells are selected on the Army List.
The Range of these spells is set to 36".
The model cannot cast Bound Spells in the same Player Turn that it performed a Move Manoeuvre.

Name: Kingsoath
Description:
The model part gains +1 Att.
The extra Melee Attack resulting from Kingsoath can only be allocated towards targets in a unit containing a <u><link="dh-ancient_grudge">Grudge Marker</link></u>.

Name: Whirling Chains of Doom
Description: The wielder gains +1D3 <u><link="rulebook-grind_attack">Grind Attacks</link></u> when engaged with an enemy unit containing a <u><link="dh-ancient_grudge">Grudge Marker</link></u>.

Name: Runic Engravings
Description:
During Pre-game Selections, each Hold Guardians unit must select one of the following effects, with Duration: Permanent.

• +1 Str, +1 AP.
• +3 Agi, <u><link="rulebook-vanguard">Vanguard</link></u>.

Name: Forge Repeater
Description: Aim 4+, Range 18", Shots 4, Str 5, AP 2, <u><link="rulebook-flaming_attacks">Flaming Attacks</link></u>, <u><link="rulebook-quick_to_fire">Quick to Fire</link></u>

Name: Forge Gun
Description: Aim 3+, Range 18", Shots 1, Str 4, AP 0, <u><link="rulebook-march_and_shoot">March and Shoot</link></u>, <u><link="rulebook-magical_attacks">Magical Attacks</link></u>, <u><link="rulebook-stand_and_shoot">Stand and Shoot</link></u>, Always hits on 3+

Name: Shrapnel Grenades
Description: One use only. Duration: One Phase. Can be activated in the Movement Phase: when activated, the model gains <u><link="rulebook-sweeping_attack">Sweeping Attack (D3 hits, Str 3, AP 1)</link></u>.

Name: Wyrm-Slayer Rocket
Description: Aim 3+, Range 24", Shots 1, Str 6, AP 3, <u><link="rulebook-flaming_attacks">Flaming Attacks</link></u>, <u><link="rulebook-multiple_wounds">Multiple Wounds (D3)</link></u>

Name: Battle Runes
Description:
Battle Runes are <u><link="rulebook-bound_spells">Bound Spells</link></u>, selected from the list below.
Note which spells are selected on the Army List.
Unless mentioned otherwise, the Range of these Bound Spells is 18".
