In units with only one rank, a raised model can either be placed in the first rank, or the player can declare the first rank complete and create a new rank (remember that you need at least a Proper Rank of models in the first rank before creating a second rank). Any models that cannot be placed in a legal position following the Unit Spacing rule are lost. Any used one-use-only effects or destroyed equipment are not regained. Raised models are subject to the same ongoing effects as their unit.

Name: Summoned Units
Description:
Summoned units are units that are created during the game.

	When summoning a unit, all its models must be deployed within the specified range of the ability. If the unit is summoned as a result of a Ground-type spell, at least one model must be placed on the targeted point, and all models must be within the spell’s range. Summoned models must be placed following the Unit Spacing rule. If the whole unit cannot be deployed, then no models can be deployed.

Once summoned, the newly created unit operates as a normal unit controlled by the side that summoned it. Summoned units do not award Victory Points to the opponent when they are  removed as a casualty.

Name: Properties of Magic Items
Description:
The following terms are common keywords used to describe Magic Items:

  •  Magic Items Category: Each Magic Item belongs to a single Magic Item Category, listed below. No model or unit can select more than one Magic Item from each category.

  •  Dominant: A model can only have one Dominant Magic Item, no matter its category.

  •  Legendary: All Magic Items are Legendary, unless stated otherwise.

Name: Who is affected ?
Description:
Unless specifically mentioned otherwise:

  •  Global and Defensive Characteristic modifiers are applied only to the model part taking the Magic Item (and not to other model parts such as mounts)*.

  •  Model Rules that are not Attack Attributes are applied to the combined model as a whole.

  •  Offensive Characteristic modifiers and Attack Attributes are applied only to the model part.

  •  Rules and modifiers from a Weapon Enchantment are only applied to the Standard Melee Attacks made with the Enchanted Weapon.

Exceptions to the above may be noted in the item's description, using terminology such as:

  •  The wielder, wearer, bearer: These terms mean the same thing for rules purposes and refer to the model part the Magic Item was bought for, and therefore generally do not affect mounts.

  •  The wielder's model, wearer's model, bearer's model: These terms refer to all model parts of the model the Magic Item was bought for, including their mounts.

  •  The wielder's unit, wearer's unit, bearer's unit: affects all model parts in the same unit as the model the Magic Item was bought for, including mounts and the model itself.

*Characters on mounts with fixed Global or Defensive Characteristics values, i.e. anything not C or C+X, do not benefit from +X modifiers to those Characteristics.
See Character Mounts section for examples of interactions with mounts.

Name: List of Common and Shared Magic Items
Description: Magic Items fall into one of the following categories.

Name: Eldritch Inscriptions
Description: <u><link="rulebook-first_strike">First Strike</link></u> (<u><link="rulebook-hatred">Hatred</link></u>, <u><link="rulebook-zeal">Zeal</link></u>).

Name: Hero's Heart
Description: +1 Att, and always at least Str 5 and AP 2.

Name: Giant Slayer
Description: <u><link="rulebook-crush_attack">Crush Attack</link></u>. This Crush Attack gains +1 to hit against models with Height 5.

Name: King Slayer
Description:
Attached, which cannot be lost.
While there is an enemy Character in contact with the wielder's unit, the wielder gains +1 Att, +2 Str, and +2 AP.

Name: Touch of Greatness
Description: +1 Str, +1 AP.

Name: Shield Breaker
Description: +6 AP.

Name: Supernatural Dexterity
Description: +3 Def and +3 Off.

Name: Vorpal Binding
Description: Successful <u><link="rulebook-armour_saves">Armour Saves</link></u>, <u><link="rulebook-regeneration">Regeneration Saves</link></u> and <u><link="rulebook-aegis">Aegis Saves</link></u> against attacks made with Vorpal Binding must be rerolled.

Name: Death Cheater
Description: +1 Arm and <u><link="rulebook-regeneration">Regeneration (4+)</link></u>.

Name: Destiny's Call
Description:
<u><link="rulebook-aegis">Aegis (4+)</link></u>, and the wearer's model's Arm is always 3.
Note that this means it cannot be improved beyond 3.
It is still affected by Armour Penetration.

Name: Essence of Mithril
Description: The wearer's Arm is set to 5 and can never be improved beyond this.

Name: Dusk Forged
Description:
The bearer must reroll its failed <u><link="rulebook-armour_saves">Armour Saves</link></u>.
The bearer automatically fails any <link="rulebook-special_saves">Special Save</link></u>, regardless of whether using the <u><link="rulebook-shield">Shield</link></u> or not.

Name: Sigil of Protection
Description: +1 Arm.

Name: Ghostly Guard
Description: +2 Arm against non-<u><link="rulebook-magical_attacks">Magical Attacks</link></u>.

Name: Basalt Infusion
Description: +1 Arm, <u><link="rulebook-aegis">Aegis</link></u> (3+, against <u><link="rulebook-flaming_attacks">Flaming Attacks</link></u>), and the wearer automatically fails all <u><link="rulebook-regeneration">Regeneration Saves</link></u>.

Name: Warding of Unity
Description:
Models without <link="rulebook-solitary">Solitary</link></u> only.
<u><link="rulebook-attached">Attached</link></u>, which cannot be lost, and <u><link="rulebook-resistance">Resistance</link></u> (</u><link="rulebook-melee_attacks">Melee Attacks</link></u>).

Name: Wild Warding
Description: +3 Arm. The model's Armour value can never be improved beyond 5.

Name: Banner of Speed
Description: A unit containing a Banner of Speed gains +1" Charge Speed and +1" Mobility.

Name: Stalker's Standard
Description: The bearer's unit gains <u><link="rulebook-strider">Strider</link></u>.

Name: Sheltering Standard
Description: Attacks with AP 3 or less can never reduce the <u><link="rulebook-armour_saves">Armour Save</link></u> roll of the bearer's unit to worse than 6+.

Name: Banner of Courage
Description:
The bearer's unit may reroll failed <u><link="rulebook-panic_tests">Panic Tests</link></u>.
If the <u><link="rulebook-battle_standard_bearer">Battle Standard Bearer</link></u> or the <u><link="rulebook-general">General</link></u> is part of the bearer's unit, it automatically passes Panic Tests instead.

Name: Distortion Emblem
Description:
One use only.  Duration: End of Turn.
May be activated at the start of any Phase.
The bearer's unit gains <u><link="rulebook-hard_target">Hard Target (2)</link></u>.

Name: Flaming Standard
Description: One use only. Duration: End of Combat. Can be activated during Combat Choices. The bearer's unit gains Flaming Attacks until the End of Combat.

Name: Aether Icon
Description: The bearer's unit gains <u><link="rulebook-magic_resistance">Magic Resistance (1)</link></u>.

Name: Rending Banner
Description:
One use only.  Duration: End of Combat.
May be activated during Combat Choices: Rank-and-File models in the bearer's unit gain +1 AP.

Name: Mask of Mindless Violence
Description: <u><link="rulebook-rage">Rage</link></u>. All <u><link="rulebook-melee_attacks">Melee Attacks</link></u> made by or against the wearer gain +1 to hit.

Name: Mimic Cloak
Description: Stand Behind. The wearer’s model loses Bodyguard and Stubborn, cannot gain either in any way, and is never considered to fulfil the criteria of Bodyguard. In addition, the model cannot make any Supporting Attacks.

Name: Binding Scroll
Description:
One use only. Duration: One Phase
Can be activated during the opponent’s '<u><link="rulebook-siphon_the_veil">Siphon the Veil</link></u>' (Magic Phase, step 2).
When activated, choose a Caster and select one of their spells (Bound or Learned): the chosen Caster cannot voluntarily cast the selected instance of this spell, e.g. if a caster has the same spell more than one time, only a single instance cannot be cast, and this has no effect on <u><link="rulebook-miscast_table">Magical Mishap</link></u>.

Name: Scroll of a Free Mind
Description:
One use only. Duration: Permanent.
Can be activated in a friendly Magic Phase before drawing a Flux card: the bearer loses all its learned spells from its selected path granted by <u><link="rulebook-wizard_apprentice">Wizard Apprentice</link></u>, <u><link="rulebook-wizard_adept"></link>Wizard Adept</u>, <u><link="rulebook-wizard_master"></link>Wizard Master</u> or Universal Swap. (Hereditary Spells, as well as spells granted by other sources, are unaffected).
The bearer gains the same number of spells from a single other path available to the bearer during list creation.
The spells are gained starting from the first spell of the path, progressing towards the last spell of the Path. This does not change the bearer’s chosen Magic Path.

Note: by doing so, it is possible that a single Wizard gains the same spell twice, yet still not cast the same spell multiple times.

Example: a Death Cult Hierarch with Wizard Adept and Sould Conduct that selected spells #1 and #3 from Divination and swapped to 'Duty is Eternal' during <u><link="rulebook-battle_setup">Battle Setup</link></u> selects Evocation with Scroll of the Free Mind: they now know 'Whispers of the Veil' twice, as well as 'Danse Macabre', and 'Duty is Eternal'.

Name: Crown of the Wizard King
Description:
During <u><link="rulebook-pregame_selections">Pre-game Selections</link></u>, randomise a Magic Path (from all ten Paths in this book).
The bearer is a <u><link="rulebook-wizard_apprentice">Wizard Apprentice</link></u> using the randomised Path, which may not <u><link="rulebook-spell_selection">Swap any spells</link></u>.
The bearer cannot take any Magic Items nor any other upgrades that are restricted to Wizards (or any types of Wizards).

Name: Magical Heirloom
Description: The bearer knows the Hereditary Spell of its Army Book in addition to its other spells, i.e. it cannot select it during Spell Selection, and cannot replace or otherwise lose it.

Name: Talisman of Shielding
Description: <u><link="rulebook-aegis">Aegis (5+)</link></u>.

Name: Talisman of the Void
Description:
<u><link="rulebook-channel">Channel (1)</link></u>.
This item can only be used if the model's army total combined Channel is 2 or less (excluding Channel from this Magic Item).

Name: Rod of Battle
Description:
The bearer can cast a unique <u><link="rulebook-bound_spells">Bound Spell</link></u>: Rod of Battle
Casting Value (4+). Type: Range 18″, Augment. Duration: One Turn.
Effect: The target's <u><link="rulebook-melee_attacks">Melee Attacks</link></u> gain +1 to hit and <u><link="rulebook-magical_attacks">Magical Attacks</link></u>.

Name: Troll Tonic
Description: Regeneration(5+).

Name: Dragon's Brew
Description:
One use only. Duration: End of Turn.
<u><link="rulebook-breath_attack">Breath Attack</link></u> (Str 4, AP 1, <u><link="rulebook-flaming_attacks">Flaming Attacks</link></u>, <u><link="rulebook-stand_and_shoot">Stand and Shoot</link></u>).

Name: Obsidian Rock
Description: Magic Resistance (2).

Name: Potion of Str
Description: One use only. May be activated at the start of any Round of Combat. Until the end of the Player Turn, the bearer gains Crush Attack.

Name: Potion of Swiftness
Description:
One use only. Duration: Round of Combat.
+3 Agi.

Name: Potion of Healing
Description:
One use only. Duration: Instant.
The bearer's model Recovers 1 HP.

Name: Potion of Power Preservation
Description:
One use only. Duration: Instant.
May be used immediately when a Casting Roll or Dispel Roll fails, due to not rolling high enough.
Do not discard the Magic Dice used in this unsuccessful attempt.

Name: Scroll of Power
Description:
One use only. Duration: Instant.
May be used immediately after a Casting Roll. The bearer may add one additional free Magic Dice to this Casting Roll. The limit of 5 Magic Dice still applies.

Name: Scroll of Draining
Description:
One use only. Duration: Instant.
May be activated in the opponent's Magic Phase, immediately after <u><link="rulebook-excess_magic">Excess Magic</link></u>: the opponent immediately discards 1 Magic Dice.

Name: Spell Scroll
Description:
One use only. Duration: One Phase.
May be activated at any time during your Magic Phase. The bearer knows the spell indicated in brackets 'X' in addition to its other spells, unless the bearer already knows it.

Name: How to Use this Document
Description: This document describes the different Paths of Magic and Special Items available for The 9th Age: Fantasy Battles and as such is to be used in conjunction with the main Rulebook. For convenience, we repeated the main rules related to spells and Special Items from the Rulebook in the corresponding sections below, along with the information needed to read the Paths.

Name: Paths of Magic
Description: Spells are cast during the Magic Phase. Most spells belong to a specific Path of Magic.

Name: Faction specific Spells
Description: Each Faction has some unique spells to cast.

Name: Spell Name
Description: Use the spell's name to indicate which spell you want to cast.

Name: Casting Value
Description: The Casting Value (CV) is the minimum number you need to roll with Magic Dice to successfully cast the spell.

Name: Spell Type
Description:
The spell type specifies how the target of the spell is chosen. A spell can have one or more of the following spell Types:

  •  Augment: Can only target friendly units, (or friendly models within units if Focused).

  •  Aura: Targets all eligible targets. For example, a spell with Augment, Aura, and Range 12" targets all friendly units within 12" of the Caster.

  •  Caster: Can only target the model casting the spell.

  •  Damage: Can only target units not Engaged in Combat.

  •  Focused: Can only target single models, including Attachable Models inside units. If a model consisting of multiple model parts is targeted, only one part can be chosen.

  •  Ground: The spell targets a point on the Battlefield rather than units or models.

  •  Hex: Can only target enemy units, (enemy models within units if Focused).

  •  Missile: Can only target units within the Caster's Line of Sight. It cannot be cast if the Caster is Engaged in Combat. When casting a spell with Type: Missile, check which is the Target Facing (see Shooting Phase chapter).

  •  Range X": The spell has a maximum casting range. Targets must be within X" of the Caster.

  •  Universal: The spell can target both friendly and enemy units, (or models within units if Focused).

Name: Duration
Description: All spells have a Duration, which follows the same rule as described under Duration of Effects, see Definitions and Terminology chapter.

Name: Effect
Description: The effect of a spell describes what happens to the target when the spell is successfully cast.

Name: Recommended Army Size and Table Size
Description: This Season Pack was prepared for a Field Army of 4000 points to be used on a 48"x72" Battlefield.

Name: Deployment Types
Description:
Follow the rules in the Reference Handbook, except where those rules are overridden by the details under each Deployment Type.
Unless mentioned otherwise, the 'Central Line' is the line drawn through the Battlefield Centre and parallel to the Long Battlefield Edges.

<u><link="season-frontline_clash">4.1. Frontline Clash</link></u>
<u><link="season-bottleneck">4.2. Bottleneck</link></u>
<u><link="season-spearhead">4.3. Spearhead</link></u>
<u><link="season-spearhead">4.4. Encircle</link></u>
<u><link="season-refused_flank">4.5. Refused Flank</link></u>
<u><link="season-assault_at_first_light">4.6. Assault at First Light</link></u>

Name: Frontline Clash
Description: Deployment Zones are more than 12" away from the Central Line, and more than 6" from the Short Battlefield Edges.

Name: Bottleneck
Description:
Player A’s Deployment Zone is more than 9" away from the Central Line, and more than 12" from the Short Battlefield Edges.
Player B’s Deployment Zone is more than 15" away from the Central Line.
The Defender also chooses if they are player A or player B.

Name: Spearhead
Description:
Deployment Zones are more than 15" away from the Central Line.
The table is divided along the Long Battlefield Edge into three equal areas (‘west’, ‘centre’, ‘east’).
The Defender chooses one of those three areas to be their ‘Spearhead Area’.
The Attacker then immediately chooses one of the remaining two areas as their Spearhead Area.
Units Deployed entirely within their Spearhead Area gain Vanguard (3 Mob).

Name: Encircle
Description:
Each Deployment Zone alternates between 9" and 15" from the Central Line.
Along with selecting a side, the Defender selects one of the two Deployment Zones.
The Attacker gets the other Deployment Zone.

Name: Refused Flank
Description:
Deployment Zones are more than 9" away from the Central Line, which is the line drawn from one corner of the Battlefield to the opposite corner.
The Defender chooses which corners to use.

Name: Assault at First Light
Description:
After choosing a side, the Defender selects a Short Battlefield Edge; the Attacker has the opposite one.
The Defender's Deployment Zone is more than 6" away from the Central Line, and 18" away from the Attacker's Short Battlefield Edge.
The Attacker's Deployment Zone is instead more than 12" away from the Central Line, and 18" away from the Defender's Short Battlefield Edge.

Name: Determining Primary and Secondary Objectives
Description:
If the Primary Objective and/or Secondary Objectives are not already determined (e.g. due to players agreeing to play particular ones, or playing in a tournament where these are already set for each game), we recommend consulting the lists below:

<u>Primary Objective:</u> roll a D6 to determine which Primary Objective to play:

5.1. <u><link="season-trophies_of_war">Trophies of War</link></u>
5.2. <u><link="season-divide_and_conquer">Divide and Conquer</link></u>
5.3. <u><link="season-breakthrough">Breakthrough</link></u>
5.4. <u><link="season-secure_targets">Secure Targets</link></u>
5.5. <u><link="season-hold_the_centre">Hold the Centre</link></u>
5.6. <u><link="season-skull_of_harald_redstub">Skull of Harald Redstub</link></u>

If the players are tied for End of Game Objective Completion, neither player wins it.

<u>Random Selection of Secondary Objective</u>
At the begining of 'Determine Secondary Objectives' (Battle Preparations- 3.B) randomise three different Secondary Objectives from the list below.
During each players 'Pre-Game Selections' (Battle Preparations- 3.C and 3.D), they select one of the three previously determined Secondary Objectives.
The Attacker cannot select one the Defender has selected.

6.1. <u><link="season-settle_the_score">Settle the Score</link></u>
6.2. <u><link="season-demonstrate_superiority">Demonstrate Superiority</link></u>
6.3. <u><link="season-slay_the_beast">Slay the Beast</link></u>
6.4. <u><link="season-assassinate">Assassinate</link></u>
6.5. <u><link="season-capture_their_banners">Capture their Banners</link></u>
6.6. <u><link="season-conserve_our_numbers">Conserve our Numbers</link></u>

If an ArmyList does not contain enough units that fulfil the requirements of a Secondary Objective, the relevant player first selects all units that fulfil the criteria and can then choose any units for the remaining choices.

Name: Primary Objectives
Description:
Roll a D6 to determine which Primary Objective to play:

5.1. <u><link="season-trophies_of_war">Trophies of War</link></u>
5.2. <u><link="season-divide_and_conquer">Divide and Conquer</link></u>
5.3. <u><link="season-breakthrough">Breakthrough</link></u>
5.4. <u><link="season-secure_targets">Secure Targets</link></u>
5.5. <u><link="season-hold_the_centre">Hold the Centre</link></u>
5.6. <u><link="season-skull_of_harald_redstub">Skull of Harald Redstub</link></u>

If the players are tied for End of Game Objective Completion, neither player wins it.

Name: Secondary Objectives
Description:
At the begining of 'Determine Secondary Objectives' (Battle Preparations- 3.B) randomise three different Secondary Objectives from the list below.
During each players 'Pre-Game Selections' (Battle Preparations- 3.C and 3.D), they select one of the three previously determined Secondary Objectives.
The Attacker cannot select one the Defender has selected.

6.1. <u><link="season-settle_the_score">Settle the Score</link></u>
6.2. <u><link="season-demonstrate_superiority">Demonstrate Superiority</link></u>
6.3. <u><link="season-slay_the_beast">Slay the Beast</link></u>
6.4. <u><link="season-assassinate">Assassinate</link></u>
6.5. <u><link="season-capture_their_banners">Capture their Banners</link></u>
6.6. <u><link="season-conserve_our_numbers">Conserve our Numbers</link></u>

If an ArmyList does not contain enough units that fulfil the requirements of a Secondary Objective, the relevant player first selects all units that fulfil the criteria and can then choose any units for the remaining choices.

Name: Trophies of War
Description:
<u>Determine and Setup Victory Conditions:</u> none.

<u>Rules:</u> whenever an Enemy unit Engaged in Combat, including all Attached Models, is removed as a casualty, put a single Trophy Token on a single Friendly Scoring unit that was just Engaged with the removed unit. A unit immediately loses all its Trophy Tokens if it loses Scoring.

<u>End of Game Completion:</u> the player with the most Trophy Tokens on their units wins. If both players have no Tokens and one player also has no Scoring units remaining on the Battlefield, the other player wins.

Name: Divide and Conquer
Description:
<u>Determine and Setup Victory Conditions:</u> mark the Battlefield Centre and divide the Battlefield into quarters.

<u>Rules:</u> END of Game Turns 3 to 6, a player gains a single Objective Counter if they have more quarters touching Friendly Scoring units than the opponent has. Each unit can only touch a single quarter at a time, determined by the location of its Centre. A unit with its Centre within 9" of the Battlefield Centre does not count as being in contact with any quarter.

<u>End of Game Completion:</u> the player with the most Objective Counters wins.

Name: Breakthrough
Description:
<u>Determine and Setup Victory Conditions:</u> mark all Scoring units on the opponent’s Army List.

<u>Rules:</u> none.

<u>End of Game Completion:</u> combine the number of:

1. marked Enemy units that were removed as a casualty whilst Engaged in Combat and
2. Friendly Scoring units in Contact with the Enemy’s Deployment Zone.

The player with the highest total wins.

Name: Secure Targets
Description:
<u>Determine and Setup Victory Conditions:</u> place two markers on opposite sides of the Battlefield, with their centres on the Central Line and 18"* from the Battlefield Centre.

<u>Rules:</u> END of Game Turns 3 to 6, a player gains a single Objective Counter if they control more markers than their opponent. A marker is controlled if there are any Friendly Scoring units and no Enemy Scoring units within 6" of a marker.

<u>End of Game Completion:</u> the player with the most Objective Counters wins.

* For Battlefields of a larger or smaller size than the standard 72"×48", replace 18" with a distance equal to a quarter of the Long Battlefield Edge.

Name: Hold the Centre
Description:
<u>Determine and Setup Victory Conditions:</u> mark the Battlefield Centre.

<u>Rules:</u> END of Game Turns 3 to 6, the player with the most Scoring units within 9" of the Battlefield Centre gains a single Objective Counter.

<u>End of Game Completion:</u> the player with the most Objective Counters wins.

Name: Skull of Harald Redstub
Description:
<u>Determine and Setup Victory Conditions:</u> place a marker on the Battlefield Centre and two markers on opposite sides of the Battlefield, with their centres on the Central Line and 18"* from the Battlefield Centre. Then place a single Clue Token on each Terrain Feature except Impassable Terrain.

<u>Rules:</u> End of each Game Turn, pick up a Clue Token from each Terrain Feature where:

 • there is a Clue Token,
 • a Friendly Scoring unit is in Contact, and
 • no Enemy Scoring units are in Contact.

END of Game Turn 3, after picking up Clue Tokens, the player with the most Clue Tokens selects which marker holds the Skull. In case of a tie, the marker at the Battlefield Centre is selected.

<u>End of Game Completion:</u> the player with the most Scoring units within 6" of the Skull wins.

* For Battlefields of a larger or smaller size the standard 72"x48", replace 18" with a distance equal to a quarter of the Long Battlefield Edge.

Name: Settle the Score
Description:
<u>Pre-game Selections:</u> on the opponent’s Army List mark any TWO Rank-and-File units.

<u>End of Game Completion:</u> BOTH marked units are removed as a casualty, Decimated,or Shaken.

Name: Demonstrate Superiority
Description:
<u>Pre-game Selections:</u> on the opponent’s Army List mark the TWO most expensive Scoring units.

<u>End of Game Completion:</u> at least one of the marked units is removed as a casualty, Decimated, or Shaken.

Name: Slay the Beast
Description:
<u>Pre-game Selections:</u> on the opponent’s Army List mark the TWO most expensive Non-Scoring Rank-and-File units or Characters of Height 4-5 with Solitary.

<u>End of Game Completion:</u> at least one of the marked units is removed as a casualty, Decimated, or Shaken.

Name: Assassinate
Description:
<u>Pre-game Selections:</u> on the opponent’s Army List mark TWO Characters. These must be: Characters who can be Deployed during Battle Setup. (Note: you cannot mark Hidden Characters.)

<u>End of Game Completion:</u> at least one of the marked units is removed as a casualty, Decimated, or Shaken.

Name: Capture their Banners
Description:
<u>Pre-game Selections:</u> on the opponent’s Army List mark ALL Scoring units and The Battle Standard Bearer. At least 3 units must be marked.

<u>End of Game Completion:</u> three or more of the marked units are removed as a casualty, Decimated, or Shaken.

Name: Conserve our Numbers
Description:
<u>Pre-game Selections:</u> on YOUR Army List the opponent marks ALL Scoring units and The Battle Standard Bearer. At least 3 units must be marked.

<u>End of Game Completion:</u> three or more of the marked units are NEITHER removed as acasualty,nor Decimated, nor Shaken.

Description: Additional characteristic on their statline which can interact with some other game effects
